description: Industrial safety real-time monitoring system — 6 Docker services (Go + React), MQTT H/W gateway, HLS CCTV streaming, KakaoTalk/SMS crisis alerts, mobile-first web UI

userStories.title: Docker Compose base setup with all 6 services
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the docker-compose.yml fully configured with all 6 service containers, networking, and volumes so that the development environment is ready.
acceptanceCriteria: docker-compose.yml defines hw-gateway, cctv-adapter, streaming, notifier, web-backend, web-frontend services
All services share sentinel-net bridge network
web-backend has db-data volume mounted at /data
web-frontend exposes ports 80/443
Each service has a Dockerfile with a basic Go or Node build (compiles but no logic yet)
docker compose up -d starts all containers without errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Go services on :8080 with /healthz. Frontend via Vite+React+TS served by nginx. Port 3080 externally (80 taken by gateway).

userStories.title: REST API spec document (web-backend)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a complete REST API specification so that frontend and backend can be developed in parallel.
acceptanceCriteria: API spec document created at docs/api-rest.md
Covers all endpoint groups: auth, contacts, sites, cameras, incidents, links, equipment, alarms
Each endpoint has: method, path, request body schema, response schema, status codes, auth requirements
Follows RESTful conventions consistently

userStories.title: WebSocket event spec document
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a WebSocket event specification so that real-time client communication is well-defined.
acceptanceCriteria: WebSocket spec document created at docs/api-websocket.md
Defines event types: crisis_alert, equipment_status, system_alarm
Each event has payload schema with examples
Defines connection lifecycle: connect, authenticate, heartbeat, reconnect

userStories.title: MQTT topic spec document (hw-gateway)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an MQTT topic specification so that H/W and S/W teams have a clear contract.
acceptanceCriteria: MQTT spec document created at docs/api-mqtt.md
Covers topics: safety/{siteId}/alert, safety/{siteId}/heartbeat, safety/{siteId}/cmd/restart
Each topic has: direction, QoS level, payload schema with examples, publish frequency

userStories.title: Inter-service HTTP API spec document
userStories.description: As a developer, I need internal HTTP API specs so that services can communicate with a clear contract.
acceptanceCriteria: Internal API spec document created at docs/api-internal.md
Covers: hw-gateway to notifier, hw-gateway to web-backend, notifier to web-backend, streaming to web-backend
Each endpoint has: method, path, request/response schema, error responses

userStories.title: MQTT client with crisis signal and heartbeat subscription
userStories.description: As the system, I need hw-gateway to connect to the MQTT broker and receive crisis/heartbeat signals so that S/W can react to H/W events.
acceptanceCriteria: hw-gateway connects to MQTT broker (configurable address via env var)
Subscribes to safety/+/alert and safety/+/heartbeat topics
On alert message, sends HTTP POST to notifier /api/notify with parsed payload
On alert message, sends HTTP POST to web-backend /api/incidents with parsed payload
Auto-reconnects on MQTT broker disconnect with exponential backoff
Logs all received messages and forwarding results
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added mosquitto broker to docker-compose. Uses paho.mqtt.golang v1.5.0. Forwards to notifier and web-backend in parallel.

userStories.title: Restart command MQTT publish
userStories.description: As a user, I want to restart equipment remotely so that production can resume after a crisis is resolved.
acceptanceCriteria: hw-gateway exposes HTTP POST /api/restart endpoint
Receives {siteId, deviceId} payload from web-backend
Publishes restart command to safety/{siteId}/cmd/restart MQTT topic
Returns success/failure response to caller
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: POST /api/restart receives {siteId, deviceId, requestedBy, reason}, publishes to safety/{siteId}/cmd/restart with QoS 1. Validates required fields, checks MQTT connection.

userStories.title: Heartbeat equipment status in-memory management
userStories.description: As the system, I need to track equipment alive/dead status based on heartbeat messages so that users can see which devices are responsive.
acceptanceCriteria: Stores last heartbeat timestamp per device in memory
Marks device as dead if no heartbeat received within configurable timeout (default 30s)
Exposes HTTP GET /api/equipment/status returning all device statuses
Status includes: deviceId, siteId, alive (bool), lastHeartbeat (timestamp)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In-memory map with sync.RWMutex keyed by siteId:deviceId. Background goroutine checks every 5s. Timeout configurable via HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_SEC env var (default 30s).

userStories.title: CCTV adapter camera connection and stream forwarding
userStories.description: As the system, I need cctv-adapter to connect to cameras via RTSP and forward raw H.264 streams to the streaming server.
acceptanceCriteria: Camera list loaded from configuration file (JSON or env)
Connects to cameras via RTSP URL (supports multiple simultaneous connections)
Forwards raw H.264 streams to streaming server without any transcoding
Per-camera health status (connected/disconnected/reconnecting) via HTTP GET /api/cameras/status
Handles camera disconnect with automatic reconnection
Adding/removing cameras requires only config change + restart
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Uses FFmpeg subprocess per camera for RTSP→RTMP forwarding (-c copy, no transcoding). Config from /config/cameras.json. Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff. Pushes to rtmp://streaming:1935/live/{cameraId}.

userStories.title: Streaming server HLS serving
userStories.description: As a user, I need to watch live camera feeds in a browser via HLS so that I can monitor the site.
acceptanceCriteria: Receives H.264 streams from cctv-adapter
Remuxes to HLS (m3u8 + ts segments) without transcoding
Serves streams at /live/{cameraId}/index.m3u8
HLS segment duration is 2-3 seconds for low latency
Old segments auto-cleaned to prevent disk fill
HTTP GET /api/streams returns list of available HLS URLs
Multiple cameras served simultaneously
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Hybrid container: nginx-rtmp (RTMP→HLS on :1935, HTTP on :8080) + Go API (:8081 for /healthz and /api/streams). hls_nested on creates /tmp/hls/{cameraId}/ dirs. 2s fragments, auto-cleanup. Stream considered active if playlist modified within 15s.

userStories.title: KakaoTalk 알림톡 API integration
userStories.description: As the system, I need to send crisis alerts via KakaoTalk 알림톡 so that responsible personnel are immediately notified.
acceptanceCriteria: Integrates with KakaoTalk 알림톡 API (credentials via env vars)
Sends templated message with: crisis description, site address, manager info, temporary CCTV link
Sends to all contacts fetched from web-backend GET /api/contacts
Parallel sending to multiple contacts
Logs success/failure per contact
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: POST /api/notify receives alert, dispatches async. Fetches contacts from web-backend, requests temp CCTV link (degraded if fails), sends KakaoTalk to all contacts in parallel. Credentials via KAKAO_API_URL/KEY/SENDER_KEY env vars.

userStories.title: SMS fallback via NHN Cloud
userStories.description: As the system, I need SMS fallback when KakaoTalk fails so that alerts always reach personnel.
acceptanceCriteria: On KakaoTalk failure for a contact, retries via NHN Cloud SMS API
On SMS failure, sends system alarm to web-backend POST /api/alarms
Fallback chain: KakaoTalk then SMS then Web alarm (per contact, independently)
All attempts (success/failure) logged with timestamps
NHN Cloud credentials via env vars
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fallback chain per-contact: KakaoTalk → NHN Cloud SMS → web-backend system alarm (POST /api/alarms). NHN SMS API v3.0 with X-Secret-Key auth. Credentials via NHN_SMS_APP_KEY/SECRET_KEY/SENDER_NO env vars. Both unconfigured channels skip gracefully.

userStories.title: Temporary link request and message inclusion
userStories.description: As the system, I need notification messages to include a temporary CCTV link so that recipients can immediately view the site.
acceptanceCriteria: On crisis event, requests temporary link from web-backend POST /api/links/temp
Includes the temporary link URL in KakaoTalk/SMS message body
Link is a full URL pointing to /view/{token} on web-frontend
If link request fails, still sends notification without link (degraded)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Notifier constructs full /view/{token} URL using FRONTEND_URL env var (default http://localhost:3080). Token comes from web-backend POST /api/links/temp response. Degraded mode (no link) if request fails.

userStories.title: SQLite schema initialization and migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the database schema to be created automatically on first run so that the system is self-initializing.
acceptanceCriteria: SQLite database created at /data/sentinel.db on first run
Tables created: contacts, cameras, sites, incidents, users
Schema matches data model in AGENTS.md (id, name, phone for contacts, etc.)
Migration mechanism supports adding columns/tables in future versions
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Pure Go SQLite via modernc.org/sqlite v1.34.5 (no CGO). Version-based migration mechanism with _migrations tracking table. Tables: users, contacts, cameras, sites, incidents. WAL mode + foreign keys enabled. DB at /data/sentinel.db (configurable via DB_PATH env var).

userStories.title: User registration API
userStories.description: As a new user, I need to register an account so that I can request access to the system.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /auth/register accepts username, password, name
Creates user with status pending
Returns 201 with user ID (no token — not yet approved)
Duplicate username returns 409
Password is hashed before storage (bcrypt)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: POST /auth/register with bcrypt password hashing. Validates username, password (min 8 chars), name. Returns 201 with pending status. 409 for duplicate username. golang.org/x/crypto v0.31.0 added for bcrypt.

userStories.title: Login and JWT authentication
userStories.description: As an approved user, I need to log in and receive a JWT token so that I can access protected endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /auth/login accepts username + password
Returns JWT token for approved users only
JWT contains: userId, role (admin/user), expiry
Unapproved (pending) users get 403 with pending approval message
Invalid credentials return 401
Built-in admin account created on first run (configurable credentials via env vars)
Auth middleware validates JWT on all /api/* endpoints
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: POST /auth/login with bcrypt verification. JWT (HS256, 24h expiry) with userId+role claims via github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5. Auth middleware on all /api/* routes. Built-in admin seeded on first run (ADMIN_USERNAME/ADMIN_PASSWORD env vars, defaults admin/sentinel1234). JWT_SECRET env var (random if unset).

userStories.title: Admin approval flow
userStories.description: As an admin, I need to approve or reject pending user registrations so that only authorized people can access the system.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /auth/pending (admin only) lists users with status pending
POST /auth/approve/{userId} (admin only) sets status to active
POST /auth/reject/{userId} (admin only) sets status to rejected
Non-admin users get 403 on these endpoints
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: requireAdmin() helper validates JWT inline for /auth/* admin routes. GET /auth/pending returns pending users sorted by created_at. Approve/reject update status with 404 for non-existent/non-pending users. Uses Go 1.22 PathValue for {userId} extraction.

userStories.title: Contacts CRUD API
userStories.description: As an admin, I need to manage notification target contacts so that the right people receive crisis alerts.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/contacts returns list of all contacts
POST /api/contacts creates a contact (name, phone required)
PUT /api/contacts/{id} updates a contact
DELETE /api/contacts/{id} removes a contact
Phone number format validation
Admin or authorized user only
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GET /api/contacts (any auth user), POST/PUT/DELETE admin-only. Phone validation via regex (01X-XXXX-XXXX). PUT supports partial updates (name or phone). DELETE returns 204. contacts.go file with 4 handlers.

userStories.title: Site info management API
userStories.description: As an admin, I need to manage site information so that crisis alerts include correct location details.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/sites returns site info (address, manager_name, manager_phone)
PUT /api/sites/{id} updates site info
Admin only
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GET /api/sites and PUT /api/sites/{id}, both admin-only. PUT supports partial updates (address, managerName, managerPhone). Phone validation reuses existing phoneRegex. Follows contacts.go pattern.

userStories.title: Temporary link create/verify/revoke
userStories.description: As the system, I need JWT-based temporary links for CCTV access so that external viewers can watch without logging in.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/links/temp generates JWT token with 24h expiry, returns full URL
GET /api/links/verify/{token} validates token and returns camera access info
DELETE /api/links/{id} adds token to in-memory blacklist (forced revoke)
GET /api/links lists active temporary links (admin only)
Expired and blacklisted tokens return 401
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In-memory store with sync.RWMutex. UUID v4 link IDs. Separate TempLinkClaims JWT type (linkId field). POST /api/links/temp on main mux (accepts admin JWT or no auth for internal calls). GET /api/links/verify/{token} public. GET /api/links and DELETE /api/links/{id} on apiMux (admin only). Blacklist by linkID. FRONTEND_URL env var for URL construction.

userStories.title: Crisis event WebSocket push
userStories.description: As a user viewing the web UI, I need real-time crisis alerts so that I'm immediately aware of incidents.
acceptanceCriteria: WebSocket endpoint at /ws
Clients authenticate via JWT query param on connection
On POST /api/incidents from hw-gateway, broadcasts crisis_alert event to all connected clients
Event payload includes: incident details, timestamp, site info
Handles client disconnect/reconnect gracefully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: WebSocket hub with gorilla/websocket v1.5.3. JWT auth via ?token= query param (supports both user JWT and temp link JWT). POST /api/incidents on main mux (internal, no auth). Broadcasts crisis_alert to all connected clients. Ping/pong keepalive (30s interval). Per-client send buffer with graceful disconnect.

userStories.title: Restart command API (forward to hw-gateway)
userStories.description: As a user, I need a REST endpoint to trigger equipment restart so the web frontend can send restart commands.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/equipment/restart accepts {siteId, deviceId}
Forwards to hw-gateway POST /api/restart
Returns hw-gateway response to client
Admin or authorized user only
Logs restart request with user info
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: POST /api/equipment/restart on apiMux (any authenticated user). Forwards {siteId, deviceId, requestedBy, reason} to hw-gateway POST /api/restart. Logs user info. HW_GATEWAY_URL env var (default http://hw-gateway:8080).

userStories.title: Camera list and HLS URL API
userStories.description: As a user, I need to know which cameras are available and their stream URLs so I can watch live feeds.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/cameras returns camera list from DB with HLS URLs fetched from streaming service
Each camera includes: id, name, location, zone, hlsUrl, status (connected/disconnected)
Caches streaming URLs with short TTL to avoid excessive internal calls
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GET /api/cameras on apiMux (any authenticated user). Fetches cameras from DB, enriches with HLS URLs from streaming GET /api/streams and status from cctv-adapter GET /api/cameras/status. 10s TTL cache for internal service responses. STREAMING_URL and CCTV_ADAPTER_URL env vars added to docker-compose.

userStories.title: Incident history API
userStories.description: As a user, I need to view past crisis incidents so I can review what happened and when.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/incidents returns paginated incident list (default 20 per page)
Supports query params: page, limit, from (date), to (date)
Each incident includes: id, occurred_at, confirmed_at, confirmed_by
Sorted by occurred_at descending (newest first)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Bottom tab bar layout (mobile-first)
userStories.description: As a user, I need a mobile-friendly tab navigation so I can quickly switch between main sections.
acceptanceCriteria: Bottom tab bar with 4 tabs: CCTV, Incidents, Management, Settings
Active tab visually highlighted
Tab switches content area without page reload
Responsive: works on mobile (primary) and desktop (secondary)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: CCTV multi-view with camera switching
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see multiple camera feeds simultaneously and tap to enlarge one so I can monitor the site effectively.
acceptanceCriteria: Grid layout showing all camera HLS streams (2x2 or list on mobile)
HLS playback via HLS.js (or native on Safari)
Tap/click a camera to enlarge to full-width
Camera name and zone label overlaid on each feed
Disconnected overlay when stream is unavailable
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Crisis alert banner (WebSocket)
userStories.description: As a user, I need to see a visible alert when a crisis occurs so I can respond immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Connects to web-backend WebSocket on app load
On crisis_alert event, shows persistent red banner at top of screen
Banner shows: timestamp, brief description
Banner is dismissible but stays until manually dismissed
Reconnects WebSocket automatically on disconnect
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Contact management UI
userStories.description: As an admin, I need to add, edit, and remove notification contacts so I control who gets crisis alerts.
acceptanceCriteria: List view showing all contacts (name, phone)
Add contact form with name and phone fields
Edit inline or via modal
Delete with confirmation dialog
Phone number format validation on input
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Site info management UI
userStories.description: As an admin, I need to view and edit site information so that alerts include correct details.
acceptanceCriteria: Display current site info: address, manager name, manager phone
Edit mode with save/cancel
Validation on required fields
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Temporary link create/manage UI
userStories.description: As an admin, I need to create and manage temporary CCTV links to share with external parties.
acceptanceCriteria: Create link button generates new temporary link
Shows copyable URL after creation
List of active links with creation time and expiry
Revoke button per link with confirmation
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Account management UI (admin only)
userStories.description: As an admin, I need to view and approve/reject pending user registrations.
acceptanceCriteria: List of pending registrations with name and username
Approve and reject buttons per user
List of active users
Only visible to admin role
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Equipment restart button (2-step confirmation)
userStories.description: As a user, I need to restart equipment remotely with safety confirmation to prevent accidental restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: Restart button visible on CCTV or equipment status area
First click: confirmation dialog asking Are you sure you want to restart?
Second click: final confirm with optional reason text input
Sends restart request to web-backend API
Shows success/failure result
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: 119 emergency call button with location
userStories.description: As a user, I need to quickly call 119 emergency services with location information during a crisis.
acceptanceCriteria: Prominent 119 button on crisis alert banner and CCTV page
On tap: requests browser Geolocation permission
Shows confirmation with site address before dialing
Initiates tel:119 call on confirmation
Falls back to site address from settings if geolocation unavailable
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Viewer-only page /view/{token}
userStories.description: As an external viewer with a temporary link, I need to view CCTV feeds without logging in.
acceptanceCriteria: Route /view/{token} validates JWT token via web-backend API
Valid token: shows CCTV multi-view (same as main CCTV tab)
Expired/invalid token: shows error message with no access
No tab bar, no management UI — CCTV player only
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Incident history page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to review past crisis incidents to understand patterns and response times.
acceptanceCriteria: List of incidents with: date/time, confirmed status, confirmed by
Paginated (load more or infinite scroll)
Date range filter
Sorted newest first
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Login and registration UI
userStories.description: As a user, I need login and registration pages so I can access or request access to the system.
acceptanceCriteria: Login page with username + password fields
Registration page with username, password, name fields
Login redirects to CCTV tab on success
Registration shows pending approval message on success
Error messages for invalid credentials / duplicate username
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Crisis E2E — Mock H/W to notification receipt
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify the full crisis flow end-to-end to ensure all services work together.
acceptanceCriteria: Mock MQTT publisher sends alert to safety/site1/alert
hw-gateway receives and forwards to notifier and web-backend
notifier requests temp link from web-backend
notifier sends KakaoTalk (or mock) with temp link
web-backend broadcasts WebSocket event to connected clients
Incident record created in database
Full chain completes within 5 seconds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Restart E2E — Web button to H/W mock receipt
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify the full restart flow end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Web frontend restart button triggers POST to web-backend
web-backend forwards to hw-gateway
hw-gateway publishes to MQTT safety/site1/cmd/restart
Mock MQTT subscriber receives restart command with correct payload
Typecheck passes
